task_name: FlexDex Mechanical Proof of Concept - Surgical Tool Innovation Presentation
classification_rationale: This is an educational/instructional presentation about a novel surgical instrument (FlexDex) with mechanical proof-of-concept demonstrations. It contains title slides, narration context, and educational diagrams showing degrees of freedom and technical specifications. No FLS standardized task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, or Suture tasks) is being performed. The content is designed to teach about instrument capabilities, not to score trainee performance on an FLS objective.

demonstrated_techniques: Six degrees of freedom surgical tool design
One-to-one motion mapping between handle and end-effector
Compliant transmission strips for force transmission
Intuitive hand-like actuation in minimally invasive format
verbal_cues: Titles indicate this is a mechanical proof of concept for enhanced degrees of freedom
Diagrams label each degree of freedom explicitly
Presentation authored by Shorya Awtar (Mechanical Engineering), James D. Geiger (Surgery), and Tristan Trutna (Ph.D. candidate) from University of Michigan
common_errors_addressed: Traditional laparoscopic tools have only four degrees of freedom — this design expands to six
The presentation contrasts traditional four-DOF mapping with the enhanced wrist-like capabilities of FlexDex
teaching_content.context: This is a mechanical engineering innovation presentation showcasing advanced surgical tool design. It is NOT an FLS task performance video and therefore should NOT be scored using FLS rubrics.

summary: This video is an educational presentation of the FlexDex surgical tool concept. It demonstrates mechanical principles and degrees of freedom through diagrams and prototype handling, but does NOT contain a standardized FLS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, or Suture). No performance score is applicable. The content is appropriate for extracting teaching materials about advanced surgical instrument design but falls outside the scope of FLS task assessment.
